title: euphrasia minima subsp. pulchella
intro: Euphrasia minima subsp. pulchella, also known as the small or alpine eye-catchers, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine due to its rich content of iridoids, which are known for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Euphrasia minima subsp. pulchella include its ability to soothe and protect the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in eye salves and infusions, and it can be found in some eye care products, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
